findings.evidence: The closing reaches Elise's act of betrayal and then retreats into report-summary instead of staging the incriminating act itself: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:289; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:291; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:293; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:295; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:299.
findings.problem: This is sanitization drift at the chapter's decisive moral beat. The scene arrives at the exact moment when Elise should have to write, stop, alter, or conceal a concrete sentence that would send police into the quarry, but the prose backs off into thematic paraphrase about what the report would mean. That summary softens the bodily and procedural reality of betrayal just when the chapter most needs presence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the closing beat in the alcove at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:299 so Elise physically begins the report and confronts one or two exact incriminating facts on the page. Good options: let her write the entrance street, Lucien's name, or a child-related detail, then stop, strike through, fold the paper away, or break off mid-line. Keep the choice in scene and in hand, not in retrospective thematic gloss.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised closing anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:289 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:299 shows at least one concrete report sentence written or half-written in the alcove and Elise's refusal is conveyed by an observable action on the page; FAIL if the passage still summarizes what the report would do without showing her materially begin and interrupt it.

findings.evidence: Nadir catches Elise taking stock of the print room and the room tightens, but that suspicion leaves almost no bruise before Lucien offers deeper access: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:151; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:157; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:273; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:279.
findings.problem: The chapter's sharpest tactical danger resolves too cleanly. Elise is explicitly read as someone 'taking stock,' but by supper the quarry's leader is offering her tomorrow's print-room work with no visible condition, rebuke, or social residue from the suspicion beat. That keeps the infiltration plot from leaving enough friction inside an otherwise strong communal chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a concrete aftereffect to the suspicion beat across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:147, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:157, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:273. Options: Nadir or Clemence can qualify the offer, Lucien can frame tomorrow's work as supervised or probationary, or Elise can notice that one resident keeps watching her through the meal. The point is not bigger conflict but visible cost and remembered suspicion.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text ties the accusation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:147 to at least one later, readable consequence before or during the offer at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:273, such as a condition on access, a pointed follow-up, or an altered social temperature; FAIL if the accusation still vanishes without changing how tomorrow's print-room access is granted.

findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly lands discovery through correction-by-negation: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:65; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:89; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:159; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:239; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:285.
findings.problem: The prose keeps telling the reader what the quarry is not before it states what it is. That pattern fits Elise's overturned assumptions once or twice, but at this frequency it becomes an editorial tic, flattening the chapter's discoveries into repeated corrections instead of letting the rendered specifics carry the turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the cited beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:239, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:285 so most of them land through affirmative image, action, or judgment rather than a 'not/no ... actually' structure. Preserve Elise's reassessment, but let the positive fact arrive first in the sentence more often than the correction.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised versions of the cited beats, at least four of those passages are readable as direct affirmative observations rather than correction openings built around 'not' or 'no'; FAIL if the same discovery moments still depend on repeated negation-first framing across those named lines.

findings.evidence: A mouth-centered reaction default recurs often enough to become visible shorthand: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:63; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:83; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:141; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:271.
findings.problem: This is exactly the kind of recurring facial-expression formula that starts reading like autopilot once it appears three or more times in a chapter. The repeated mouth-twitch/bent/moved cue narrows the ensemble's physical expressiveness and makes otherwise distinct character beats feel mechanically related.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:271 so at least two of them land through different physical or vocal detail. Use hands, shoulders, breath, timing, gaze, or line delivery rather than another mouth-based micro-expression.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited reaction beats are revised so the character's response is conveyed without a mouth-centered expression and the chapter no longer contains more than two such mouth-reaction beats; FAIL if the same four moments still rely on mouth twitching, bending, or moving as the default signal.

summary: Chapter 2 lands its core state change cleanly: Elise maps the refuge, identifies Lucien, and understands that the state means to crush a lived social world rather than a cartoon bomb cell. Continuity, past-tense control, and character dialogue register are all strong enough to pass. It still fails on craft and prose because the closing moral decision is summarized instead of enacted, the strongest suspicion beat leaves too little tactical residue, and visible prose tics (negation-first correction and mouth-reaction shorthand) start to flatten otherwise sharp material.
